history.summary: With 53 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes (n=50), normal BB state (n=16), 120-240s TTC (n=26), bearish-aligned MTF (n=12), sell-pressure CVD (n=19), and strong ADX (n=31) show confirmed positive EV (win-rates 0.64-0.83, avg_ev_after_cost 0.11-0.14) while UP signals and long-hold or active-volume buckets remain marginal or negative. Sample size still limits precision on most sub-buckets; overall edge is modest but directionally consistent with selectivity toward DOWN/trending conditions.

With n=53 total, DOWN_STRONG (n=26, wr=0.692), trending regime (n=50, wr=0.64), 120-240s TTC (n=26, wr=0.731), normal BB state (n=16, wr=0.875), bearish_aligned MTF (n=12, wr=0.833) and sell_pressure CVD (n=19, wr=0.737) show confirmed positive EV above breakeven after costs; UP signals, >=240s TTC, active volume and range_top are reliably negative. Most other buckets remain too small or mixed to confirm.

With n=53 settled trades the bot shows modest positive EV (overall wr 0.604, avg_ev_after_cost 0.116) driven by a handful of buckets that meet n>=8 and clear statistical thresholds; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, 120-240s TTC, normal BB state, bearish-aligned MTF, sell-pressure CVD and strong-ADX regimes are confirmed profitable while most other slices remain noisy or negative. Buckets with n>=8 show confirmed profitability in DOWN direction, bearish-aligned MTF, normal BB state, 120-240s TTC, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD and range-middle; UP signals and >=240s TTC remain negative or marginal. Most other slices have n<8 and are ignored as unreliable.

With n=53 settled trades, overall edge is modestly positive (wr 0.604, ev_after_cost 0.116) but driven by a handful of regimes; DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, normal BB, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD, bearish MTF alignment, and 120-240s TTC show confirmed positive EV with n>=8 and wr well above breakeven. Most other buckets are either n<8 noise or show flat/negative realized pnl despite positive ev. Sample remains too small for high-confidence live deployment.

With n=53 total settled signals, overall win rate 60.4% and small positive EV after costs, but only a handful of buckets meet n>=8 with win rates confidently above breakeven and positive realized PnL. DOWN_STRONG, trending/Hurst, z-score -2..1, TTC 120-240s, BB normal, volume dead, bearish HTF/aligned, strong ADX, sell-pressure CVD, and range-middle/bottom show confirmed edge; most other slices are noise or under-sampled. No material changes possible as this is the first analysis.

With n=53 total settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals in trending regimes show confirmed positive EV (wr 0.69, +15.5 pnl) while UP signals remain marginal/negative; strong patterns include ttc 120-240s, bb normal, bearish_aligned mtf, sell_pressure cvd, and zscore -2..1 with n>=13 and positive pnl/ev after costs. Many buckets remain too small or mixed to confirm, with overall edge still modest and sample-limited.

headlines: Bitcoin falls below $60,000 on track for rare back-to-back quarterly loss
Record ETF outflows hit sentiment as BTC tests support

note.summary: 111 settled trades, 62.16% win rate, +$22.47 PnL, profit factor 1.12. Severe data sparsity across all dimensional slices (n=1 everywhere) makes context analysis meaningless. DOWN bias is clear (91 down vs 20 up, 64.8% vs 50% win rates), but losses are 47% larger than wins, indicating negative skew that will compound unfavorably.

knob_recommendations.reason: All buckets show n=1; require â¥30 samples before any context claims edge
UP trades at 50% WR with 20 samples; raise bar to 0.65 predicted prob or halt UP entirely
Avg loss ($4.64) is 1.47x avg win ($3.15); reject trades where tail risk exceeds 1.3x expected win
176/183 exec rejects are underdog_price_below_floor; current floor too aggressive, test 0.25

new_lessons.rule: Do not slice performance by 9+ dimensions when total n=111; each bucket has nâ¤1, making all stats noise
DOWN side shows 64.8% WR vs UP 50%, but loss size 1.47x win size will erode edge under compounding
176/183 rejects are underdog_price_below_floor (96% of exec rejects); floor is primary bottleneck, not edge filter
Brier 0.239 vs baseline 0.25 is marginal; 0.2-0.3 bucket predicts 58% up actual, 0.3-0.4 predicts 29%, model miscalibrated

lessons.rule: TradingView 2-4min timeframe signals have no predictive edge on 5min BTC outcomes (45% vs 39% base); treat as noise, not signal
DOWN win rate (63.4%) is higher than UP (50%), but DOWN trades were 4x more frequentâthis is filter selection bias, not a tradable DOWN edge
168/277 execution gate rejections for underdog_price_below_floor indicate edge model outputs sub-viable edges; tighten model threshold or raise gate floor
Profit factor 1.0007 with avg_loss > avg_win means any transaction cost increase or slippage wipes edge; do not trade until PF > 1.05 on 500+ samples
TradingView MTF signal hit rate 45.3% (vs 38.8% base) is statistically zero; do NOT trade on directional convictionâedge is mispricing arbitrage only
DOWN: 63.9% win, +$7.71 PnL (n=83); UP: 50% win, -$6.35 PnL (n=20). Asymmetry is not alphaâit's market structure (underdog floor rejects 168 UP candidates). Exploit DOWN underdog tail only.
Brier 0.243 < baseline 0.25 but log-loss 0.683 is poor; edge_model calibration bins show no monotonic trend (0.3-0.4 bin: 31.4% empirical_up). Model is curve-fit noiseâdo NOT size by confidence.

168/175 rejects are underdog_price_below_floor; the gate is correctly filtering non-mispriced bets. The 103 accepted trades are *selection artifacts*âloosen floor to 0.25 cautiously and measure if win rate holds.
PF 1.01 on 103 trades is <1 std dev from breakeven (avg_loss $4.63 vs avg_win $2.98 suggests tail risk). Do NOT scale until PF â¥1.15 on â¥200 settled trades.
DOWN bets on 5-min BTC show 63.9% win vs 50% UP; exploit DOWN-only until UP win rate exceeds 58% over 50+ samples
TradingView UP signals hit 29%, DOWN hit 59%; treat UP signals as contrary indicator (fade or avoid) and DOWN as weak confirmation
168 UP rejections for underdog_price_below_floor vs 0 DOWN; underdog UP prices suffer adverse selection, tighten floor to 0.45+ for UP only
avg loss 1.55x avg win destroys compounding; reject any trade where model edge / (1 - model_prob) > 0.65 * (model_prob / model_edge)
profit factor 1.01 is coin-flip territory; require PF >= 1.15 sustained over 200+ trades before considering live capital allocation
DOWN signals (58.6% hit, +$7.71) have real edge; UP signals (29.2% hit, -$6.35) are anti-predictiveâtrade DOWN only
168/175 rejects for underdog_price_below_floor indicate systematic mispricing or overly conservative floor; investigate pricing vs realized outcomes
No-signal cohort (62% win, +$1.36) beats UP-signal cohort (54.2% win, -$6.35)âUP signals may be reverse indicators
5min timeframe DOWN signals: 5/7 hit (71.4% estimated), +$8.52âbest sub-context for exploitation
TradingView UP signals show 29% hit rate and -$6.35 PnL over 24 trades; disable UP signal consumption until nâ¥100 profitable sample
DOWN-side trades: 83/103 (80.6%), 63.9% win rate, +$7.71 PnL vs UP: 20/103, 50% win rate, -$6.35 PnL; exploit asymmetry
168/175 exec rejects = underdog_price_below_floor; edge model assigns >70% confidence to sub-0.20 underdog prices that fail realism
0.2-0.3 bucket: 54.5% empirical vs ~25% predicted; 0.3-0.4: 31.4% vs ~35%; recalibrate or reject edge_prob < 0.4
Avg loss $4.63 vs avg win $2.98 (1.55x); with 61% win rate, need win:loss ratio â¥1.6:1 for sustainable edge; cap losses
Down-side 64% WR (n=84) vs up 50% (n=20); investigate if DOWN setups have tighter spreads or better liquidity, or if gate logic systematically rejects weaker UP candidates
170 of 177 gate rejections are underdog_price_below_floor; if this kills profitable setups, consider dynamic floor tied to edge_quality or relax by 2 ticks
Avg loss $4.63 vs avg win $3.01 (1.54x); one bad streak erases multiple wins; add max_loss_per_trade knob or tighter stop-loss logic
All hurst/zscore/ttc buckets show n=1 or 'na'; either feature pipeline is broken or sample is too sparse to stratify; defer dimension-based rules until n>=15 per bucket
underdog_price_below_floor dominates rejects (170/177=96%); current floor too conservative, starving execution of mispriced opportunities
avg_loss ($4.63) > avg_win ($3.06); profit_factor 1.07 is fragileâstrategy needs 62%+ WR to break even, no margin for variance
Brier 0.242 â baseline 0.25, log-loss 0.68 high; model outputs probabilities but no exploitable mispricing vs marketârecalibration won't create edge
All dimension buckets have nâ¤2; 100% WR in these cells is noise, not signalârequire nâ¥30 per bucket before claiming contextual edge
85/105 DOWN trades, 64.7% WR DOWN vs 50% UP; asymmetric volume may exploit short-term mean reversion, but small win size suggests compression, not edge
underdog_price_below_floor killed 60% of gate flow; either floor is heroically saving you or choking alphaâneed A/B test
hurst=insufficient_data: 2/2 wins, +$11.11, but n=2 is noise; do NOT size up until n>=20
hurst_regime=trending: 0/1 (0% WR, -$5); avoid until 10+ samples prove otherwise
86 down vs 20 up fills; down_bias_gate passed 538 but imbalance suggests model or market skewâmonitor for drift
Brier=0.242 vs baseline=0.25 is marginal; log_loss=0.68 suggests overconfidenceâprobabilities need shrinkage
profit_factor=1.046 with avg_loss=$4.63 > avg_win=$3.06; one bad streak flips you negativeâno live until PF>1.2
max_drawdown=$43.62 vs net_pnl=$8.82 (5:1 risk/reward realized); unacceptable for liveâneed 3:1 or better
n=106 settled, but buckets have n=1-3; you need 500+ trades and >=30 per slice before claiming bucket-level edge
if execution_gate rejects >50% for underdog_price_below_floor, market data is stale or floor miscalibratedâinvestigate data feed before any floor adjustment
do not trade contexts with n<30; current max n=4 means all slice-level statistics are pure noise
avg_loss $4.64 vs avg_win $3.06 is fatal; require avg_win >= 1.5x avg_loss before claiming any edge
profit_factor 1.02 is breakeven after real costs; need >=1.5 sustained over >=200 trades to consider live readiness
zscore=-1..1: 0% WR, -$10; mean-reversion absent in noise â block trades lacking divergence signal
hurst_regime=noise: 1 trade, 0% WR, -$5; randomness dominates â require trending or insufficient_data (2/2 wins)
ttc>=240s: 66.7% WR vs <240s poor; longer resolution windows allow signal to express vs noise decay
170/177 exec rejections = underdog_price_below_floor; tight floor protects but may hide mispriced tailsâmonitor
PF 1.06, avg_loss > avg_win; edge exists but RR poor â prioritize high-conviction over volume until PF>1.3
TradingView MTF(2m/3m/4m) signals are anti-predictive for 5min BTC: 45% hit rate vs 38% base; disable in edge model
DOWN directional edge confirmed at n=90: 64% win rate, +0.27 avg PnL vs UP n=20 50% win, -0.26 avg PnL
underdog_price_below_floor rejects 96% of execution candidates (176/183); re-calibrate floor or risk zero live flow
When external signal aligned WR < opposed WR by >5pp over nâ¥50, zero signal weight and audit feature polarity
When one direction loses money (PF<1.0) over nâ¥20 while other profits, disable losing side until 30-trade revalidation
When single reject reason >60% of gate rejects, relax that constraint by 10-15% if remaining EV>0 after costs
BTC 5min DOWN bets outperform UP by 11pp WR (+$14 delta over 110 trades); bias sizing/thresholds accordingly until n=500 retest
Do not slice performance by 9+ dimensions when total n=111; each bucket has nâ¤1, making all stats noise
DOWN side shows 64.8% WR vs UP 50%, but loss size 1.47x win size will erode edge under compounding
176/183 rejects are underdog_price_below_floor (96% of exec rejects); floor is primary bottleneck, not edge filter
Brier 0.239 vs baseline 0.25 is marginal; 0.2-0.3 bucket predicts 58% up actual, 0.3-0.4 predicts 29%, model miscalibrated
